name: The Empty Belly, The Empty Bed
subtitle: Scene 1 — Home Cave
body: The clan wakes to no food in the cave — and worse, old Thag, the wisest grunting elder, is gone from his bearskin. Round footprints press into the dirt. A faint smell of singed hair hangs in the air.
Open with chaos, not exposition: a caveman grabs a fistful of nothing where breakfast should be, then finds the cold bearskin. Let them discover it.

The fork: fresh tracks lead toward the stinky water place. The footprints are too round — no toes. Nobody has the word for "spaceship." Split the seven here: trackers to the marsh (Scene 3), the rest pulled to the honey (Scene 2).

name: The Bee Thing Detour
subtitle: Scene 2 — Bee Tree
body: The food they need is a bee's nest dripping honey, halfway up a very bendy tree, swaying in the wind. This is the slapstick warm-up — laughs, not stakes.
CLIMB to reach the nest — roll a 1 and you Forget How halfway up; narrate the fall. Each round in the nest, roll RUN AWAY or take 1 from stings.
The bendy tree bows under weight — two cavemen up at once and it's a catapult. Let physics happen.
A Cave Bear is also harvesting honey and considers the tree his. If it sags, cut straight to a column of smoke over the marsh — the honey was never the point.

name: The Stinky Water Place
subtitle: Scene 3 — the marsh
body: The marsh. Tar pits ring it — instant death, only sprung when players have clear chances to avoid. Reeds. A Giant Leech in the shallows.

Here they find Thag's necklace (snagged on a reed over a tar pit — retrieving it is the avoid-the-hazard beat) and a patch of ground scorched in a perfect circle. The circle still hums faintly; anyone who licks it (someone will) tastes lightning.
A terrified Lizard Person watches the sky, delivers the reveal vaguely, then hisses and flees. The burnt circle points the way to Scene 4.

name: The Saucer Finale
subtitle: Scene 4 — The Shiny Sleep Things descend
body: A saucer descends. Out step 4–5 Ancient Astronauts with sleep-rays. Thag is inside, half-probed, being shown a diagram of a pyramid he will never understand.

They aren't hostile — every cooperative grunt fires a teaching hologram; every grab for a rock earns a sleep-ray. Cooperate/mime → they gift a "primitive battery" (does nothing useful, counts as treasure to fight over). Swarm with 7 → rays drop 2–3, the rest mob the saucer. Let chaos win.
Get probed (lose) → not death: they wake later in a field, hair singed, Thag smug.
Rescue Thag, drive off the Shiny Sleep Things, and there is your triumph. Or get probed. End on a stop, not a bow — when the saucer leaves (or crashes), the story just stops.

name: Describe Everything Vaguely
subtitle: GM reminder — tape this to your screen
body: Where another game says "a crisp dawn, birdsong, a vine-strewn wall of glowing glyphs" — you say: "Cold out. Animal noise loud. Wall of strange square rock. Some glow. Nasty shiny thing."
Throw in the 18 words whenever you can. Make the players work for the mental picture. And the instant the grunting circles — Forced Resolution. Move on. The story stops when the evening does.
